description: Python CLI tool that scans codebases for TODO/FIXME/HACK/XXX comments and generates a prioritized backlog

userStories.title: Scan repository for TODO markers
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to scan my entire repository for TODO/FIXME/HACK/XXX comments so I can see all technical debt in one place.
acceptanceCriteria: CLI accepts --root flag for repo path (default: current directory)
CLI accepts --include flag for file patterns (default: **/*)
CLI accepts --exclude flag with defaults: node_modules, target, dist, .git, build, .next
Detects TODO:, FIXME:, HACK:, XXX: markers (case-insensitive)
Extracts file path, line number, and comment text for each marker
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Deduplicate exact matches
userStories.description: As a developer, I want duplicate TODO comments to appear only once so the backlog isn't cluttered with repetition.
acceptanceCriteria: Exact text matches are deduplicated (whitespace-normalized)
First occurrence by file path alphabetically is kept
Duplicate count is tracked and shown in summary
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Cluster by module/package structure
userStories.description: As a team lead, I want TODOs grouped by module so I can assign work by area of responsibility.
acceptanceCriteria: Python: detect packages via __init__.py or directory structure
JavaScript/TypeScript: detect modules via package.json or directory structure
Java: detect packages via package declarations
Fallback: group by top-level directory if no package structure detected
Each cluster shows count of items
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Calculate priority scores
userStories.description: As a developer, I want each TODO to have a priority score so I know what to tackle first.
acceptanceCriteria: Impact score (1-5): SECURITY|DATA LOSS|P0|CRITICAL=5, BUG|FIXME=4, TODO=3, REFACTOR|CLEANUP=2, NICE TO HAVE=1
Confidence boost (+1, max 5) if comment includes issue ID pattern (#123 or ABC-123)
Priority = impact + confidence - effort (clamped 1-10)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Generate BACKLOG.md
userStories.description: As a team lead, I want a formatted BACKLOG.md file so I can review it in sprint planning meetings.
acceptanceCriteria: Summary section: total count, breakdown by marker type (TODO/FIXME/HACK/XXX)
Top 10 highest priority items section with full details
By module section: grouped clusters with item counts
Each item shows: priority score, tag, file:line, text, optional owner/date/issue ID
File written to repo root as BACKLOG.md
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Verify scan completeness
userStories.description: As a developer, I want confirmation that all TODOs were captured so I can trust the backlog.
acceptanceCriteria: After generating BACKLOG.md, re-scan repository
Compare item counts: original scan vs verification scan
Print confirmation message: '✓ Verified: captured X items'
Exit with error if counts don't match
Typecheck passes
